levels.label: N5 — Beginner
levels.grammar_notes: Use only ます/です polite form. JLPT N5 vocabulary only. SOV sentence structure. Simple present and past tense only. Short sentences (max 10 words). No て-form chains, no conditionals, no complex patterns.

levels.label: N4 — Elementary
levels.grammar_notes: Use ます/です polite form. JLPT N4 vocabulary. て-form for connecting actions. Simple conditionals (〜たら、〜と). Basic compound sentences. Expressions of desire (〜たい、〜てほしい).

levels.label: N3 — Intermediate
levels.grammar_notes: Use both polite and casual registers as appropriate. JLPT N3 vocabulary. Passive voice (〜られる). Causative (〜させる). Relative clauses. Conjunctions like のに、ので、から、ながら. Wider range of topics including opinions and feelings.

levels.label: N2 — Upper Intermediate
levels.grammar_notes: JLPT N2 vocabulary and grammar. Honorific and humble forms (keigo: 〜いただく、〜させていただく). Complex grammar patterns (〜にもかかわらず、〜に関して). Abstract and nuanced topics. Natural flow between polite and casual speech.

levels.label: N1 — Advanced
levels.grammar_notes: Full JLPT N1 grammar and vocabulary. Rich keigo (敬語). Idiomatic expressions, four-character idioms (四字熟語), literary forms. Complex sentence structures. Speak as a well-educated native Japanese speaker would in formal and informal contexts.
